source: Derived from error patterns across CB SAT practice (data/cb-corpus/)

mistakeCategories.name: Found one variable, reported it for the other
mistakeCategories.description: Student solves the system but reports x when the question wants y (or their sum).
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Asks for y; student gives the value of x.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Give the value of the OTHER variable in the solution pair.

mistakeCategories.name: Added equations without aligning coefficients
mistakeCategories.description: Student adds the two equations directly without scaling to eliminate a variable.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Adds 2x+y=5 and x+y=3 to get 3x+2y=8 and stalls or mis-solves.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Compute the answer that results from adding the equations without proper elimination.

mistakeCategories.name: Sign error during substitution
mistakeCategories.description: Student substitutes correctly but drops or flips a sign.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Substitutes y = 3 - x but uses 3 + x.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Produce the value from a single sign error in the substitution step.

mistakeCategories.name: Mishandled parallel / identical lines
mistakeCategories.description: Student gives a numeric answer when the system has no solution or infinitely many.
mistakeCategories.studentExample: Parallel lines (no solution) but student reports a specific point.
mistakeCategories.synthesisCue: Offer a specific numeric solution when the correct answer is 'no solution' or 'infinitely many'.
